user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the car is clearly visible and the bumper area can be fully inspected for damage.
claim_status_justification: The rear bumper area is visibly and severely damaged — the bumper cover is missing and the rear body panel is structurally deformed, which is consistent with and exceeds the claimed rear bumper damage.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two images show clearly different vehicles — one with severe front bumper damage and one pristine Jaguar XF — making it impossible to verify a consistent identity for the claimed vehicle and its alleged scratch.
claim_status_justification: The two submitted images depict two completely different cars with no consistent vehicle identity. One shows catastrophic front-end damage (not a scratch), and the other shows a fully undamaged Jaguar XF, making it impossible to verify the claimed front bumper scratch on a single vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two submitted images show two clearly different vehicles — a white van with a shattered windshield and a separate white car with a completely intact windshield — making it impossible to confirm which vehicle belongs to the claimant.
claim_status_justification: The image set contains two visibly different vehicles; one shows severe windshield damage and the other shows a pristine windshield on a different car, so the claimed vehicle and its damage cannot be reliably verified.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The side mirror is clearly visible in sharp focus and well-lit conditions, allowing full inspection of the mirror glass and housing for damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows the side mirror glass is shattered with multiple visible fracture lines, and the mirror appears structurally compromised, consistent with the customer's claim of being clipped while parked.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two images show two visually different vehicles (white and silver), and neither image clearly shows damage to a rear bumper — one shows a dent on a quarter panel of a white car, and the other shows an undamaged rear bumper on a silver car.
claim_status_justification: The submitted images appear to show two different vehicles, making it impossible to confirm a consistent vehicle identity. The rear bumper visible in img_2 shows no damage, and the dent in img_1 is on a quarter panel of a different-colored car — neither supports a rear bumper damage claim.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows only the rear-side of the vehicle. The front of the car and the headlight are completely outside the frame and cannot be inspected.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image does not show the front of the car or the headlight at all — the camera is aimed at the rear-side of the vehicle, making it impossible to verify or refute the claimed headlight crack.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 2 is sharp and clearly shows the door panel of a dark car at close range, providing sufficient detail to assess the claimed dent.
claim_status_justification: Image 2 clearly shows a large inward depression on the door panel consistent with a dent, directly supporting the customer's claim of a dent along the door panel area.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image contains visible Vecteezy stock photo watermarks, confirming it is not an original photograph taken by the user. It cannot be accepted as genuine personal evidence.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a minor scratch on the hood from a service visit, but the image shows a catastrophically wrecked vehicle with the hood open, fender missing, and engine exposed — far beyond any post-service scratch scenario. Additionally, the image is a watermarked stock photo, not an original user photo.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible and well-lit, allowing full inspection of the claimed damage area.
claim_status_justification: The laptop screen displays severe, clearly visible cracking radiating from a central impact point, with multiple fracture lines across the entire display panel, fully supporting the user's claim of a cracked screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the laptop hinge area in close-up detail, with the broken casing and exposed internal frame fully visible and verifiable.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the hinge area with the plastic casing cracked, separated, and exposing the internal metal frame, directly supporting the claim of a broken hinge. However, img_2 shows a completely different, undamaged laptop, suggesting the two images may not depict the same device.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop keyboard is clearly and sharply visible in close-up, with the liquid damage fully inspectable across the entire keyboard and palm rest area.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows extensive liquid (water) covering the keyboard and surrounding palm rest, confirming a water spill affecting the keyboard area as claimed. The actual damage is at least as severe as described.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 2 clearly shows the laptop lid corner area with sufficient detail to identify a visible impact dent on the surface.
claim_status_justification: Image 2 shows a visible localized impact depression on the laptop lid near the corner, consistent with the user's claim of a dent from a drop.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly and sharply visible, with the damage fully in frame and inspectable without obstruction.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a MacBook Pro screen with severe radial cracking from a central impact point, directly confirming the user's claim of screen damage/shattering.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop trackpad area and front base edge are clearly visible and well-lit, providing sufficient detail to inspect the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The trackpad surface is clearly visible and appears completely intact with no cracks, dents, scratches, or physical deformation. The user-drawn circle is a digital overlay that cannot be used as evidence of damage, and the pixels beneath it show an undamaged surface.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package corner is clearly visible and in sharp focus, allowing full inspection of the claimed damage area.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows the top corner of the cardboard box is structurally crushed inward, with the cardboard layers buckled and the tape seal split, consistent with the customer's claim of a crushed corner upon delivery.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the package exterior with severe tearing visible on the box side, providing sufficient evidence to evaluate the torn packaging claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a cardboard box with a large, jagged tear on its side exposing the inner corrugated layer, directly supporting the claim of torn packaging. However, img_2 shows a completely different, pristine and undamaged box inconsistent with the claimed damaged package.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package exterior is clearly visible and the affected surface shows obvious discoloration, warping, and material deterioration consistent with water damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows large dark wet staining, structural warping, and cardboard layer separation on the package side and corner, all consistent with significant water damage as claimed.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Missing contents cannot be verified from photos alone; the open box shows only packing material but does not confirm a product is absent, and the two images appear to show different, unrelated boxes with no clear connection.
claim_status_justification: A photograph of an open box filled with packing material cannot confirm that a specific item is missing, as contents may be concealed beneath the filler or the images may not represent the same package. Missing contents claims are inherently unverifiable by visual evidence alone.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows food cans, not a shipping box. The claimed object (crushed cardboard box) is entirely absent from the photo.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image shows food cans, not a shipping box. There is no packaging exterior or crushed box visible anywhere in the image, making it impossible to support the claim.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the package exterior with severe torn packaging damage visible at the front corner, meeting the standard for inspecting the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the package cardboard physically torn open with the tamper-evident seal broken and shredded, directly supporting the claim of a torn-open package. However, a sticky note instructing claim approval and a stock photo (img_2) were submitted alongside, raising serious integrity concerns.
